column215: What’s one thing your preferred AI chat model does especially well (compared to others you’ve tried, if any)?
column216: What’s one thing that most frustrates you when using AI chat models?
column217: What’s one feature you wish your preferred AI chat model had (or did better)?

column215: Chat gpt is generally smarter. Claude does writing tasks well. Gemini, I haven't used it a lot since I am just used to using ChatGPT, therefore I only know so much about it.
OpenAI give a good user experience with generous limits and access to all other tools like codex, sora etc.
column216: They don't learn over time. UX is still not the best.

column215: gemini create better images then gpt
column216: limitation to upload docs or creating images

column215: Claude for coding. ChatGPT for research and critical analysis. Gemini for google search.
column217: Ability to give transparency on why it is answering as such.

column215: I’m a software developer so Claude is my go-to for coding and technical tasks since it handles those really well. I use ChatGPT mostly for content creation like writing emails, documents, and also generating illustrations with Sora because it gives me consistent image results. I don’t use Gemini that much, but it’s convenient sometimes since I’m already in the Google ecosystem. For me, ChatGPT stands out because of its versatility in creative and content-focused work.
column216: What frustrates me most is having to switch between different models for different tasks. I use one for coding, another for content, and sometimes another for docs, and it would be nice if one model could handle everything well enough in one place.
column217: I wish my preferred AI model could handle both technical and creative tasks equally well so I wouldn’t have to switch between different models. It would also be great if it could keep context longer and stay more consistent across conversations, so I don’t have to repeat myself or fine-tune prompts as much.

column215: Summarize content

claude is very good at making images, or visualisations. like i recently used to create my slides. chatgpt is well chatgpt. very easy to use interface and ive been using it for 2 years so im used to it.
column216: if theyre being slow at responding or the tokens run out
column217: i wish chatgpt could generate better visualisations

column216: Speed is sometimes slow, and sometimes assumes information

column215: Understanding of concepts and summarising
column216: Lacks creativity when it comes to giving ideas
column217: Attached links that are actually valid. When it comes to referencing and citations maybe?

column215: Handles diverse queries.
column217: A better interactive study mode for making learning resources more digestible and answer specific queries within pdfs/slide decks

column215: Step by step answers
column216: Sometimes i have to repeat the prompts

column215: Gives answers in an organized manner and understands what i want
column216: They act dumb sometimes so you have to ask the same question multiple times in different ways to get desired answer

column215: It provides better analytical output compared to other LLMs
Doing analytical questions
column216: it gives wrong answers while doing quant section and moreover when asked to explain a topic just gives an overview

column215: Haven’t tried others
column216: In longer conversations, models may forget context, change tone, or misinterpret subtle instruction changes. They forget earlier instructions as well and you have to keep repeating them in the same conversation.
column217: True long-term context awareness (or long-term memory) would be incredibly useful. Even if it isn’t as deeply integrated as human memory, there should still be some level of continuity across conversations. That way, I could work on long-term projects without having to re-explain everything from scratch or repeat instructions just to get back to where I left off.

column215:
Claude gives the exact responses and follows prompt better than the other models. 
The formats it provides and the User interface is also better. In terms of analytical tasks or workflows generation, it is way better than others
column217: Claude free model doesn't allow editing the documents created and lesser free credits.

column215: Deep research on Gemini
Detailed responses especially when it comes to research. Easy integration with Visual Studio for coding
column216: Hallucinations. Models unable to connect logic between tables/code
column217: Better and faster complex visualisations for the provided data. Visualisations are not always accurate

column215: Technical research for business development purposes, seeks out information about upcoming/announced projects in the O&G sector, highly accurate as compared to chatgpt, faster response speed, understands my commands.
column216: chatgpt - info can be missed, doesn't save my history/preferences for some reason, have to explain everything to it and it will still make mistakes
column217: wish chatgpt was less of a sycophant - always seems to agree with you, even when you're wrong

column215: Has the capability to understand tasks well and execute the prompts promptly
column216: when they get stuck in circular reasoning. Or give you “fixes” that are already implemented. They don’t say “i don’t know” or stuff rather just spit unnecessary stuff
column217: Better at maths or reasoning questions ig

column215: Nothing it does particularly special but is extremely useful
column216: Can feel frustrating to use at times when the responses feel inadequate or irrelevant to what was asked

column215: open ai has the best UI as compared to its counterparts, as well as keeping my previous chats in mind while generating responses
Web search
column216: Lack of original content meaning they cheat. For example if I ask for ideas for naming a company with some background information regarding the company, they would suggest very good names but then I will find out later that they were all names of actual companies.
column217: it should tell you which sources in the training data contributed to the information it is giving you. E.g if I ask it for a marketing strategy, it should give some general ( not specific ) information on what kind of training data contributed to this type of information

column215: Give detailed responses
column217: Lift live data from the web

column215: accurate answers, gives answers that i want
speed and well-structure approach according to scenarios professional type
column216: If you are asked the same thing over and over again it makes the error worse rather than correcting it.
column217: Offline mode and local use the model without needing the internet.

column215: Helps me write more efficient code with minimal errors.

column215: Es ist echt.

The attention balance of all Mistral models is incredible compared to the alternatives - even Mistral Small running locally keeps track of multiple subjects in a conversation with very little to no confusion between them up to between 27K - 80K token context (depending on the complexity), regardless of the subject - from software development to creative writing. Mistral Medium on Le Chat is even better. The careful/arguably lower censorship (I'd argue it isn't less censored than the alternatives, the models just understand the context better so there's less false blocks on clearly harmless subjects) seems to help the model keep track of normal subjects/discussions better without becoming confused. As a Data Engineer I find this fascinating - I suspect the stricter censorship on ChatGPT 5 especially is the reason GPT5's output seems of far lower quality than ChatGPT 4 was - it's allocating too much of its attention budget on adhering to censorship rules to be able to answer normal queries without becoming confused between the rules and the users message.
column216: When they confuse different subjects within a chat. ChatGPT currently does this, as does Grok (to a lesser extent). Mistral Small and Mistral Medium are the only models that don't appear to suffer from this issue, which is very impressive.
column217:
Honestly, the current Mistral models - both local and Le Chat - are already good enough for all of my use cases.

I could wish for better memory support to get around the context limitations, but I've implemented a solution for local chats that works almost perfectly for my projects, and Le Chat (paid) and Copilot also have good implementations... So I guess less false positives on censorship (just to avoid the model confusion that seems to stem from it in current models) for the other models would be my most wished-for feature (or implement it via better model training datasets rather than UI guardrails which just cause frustration for many users...), though it's really only a ChatGPT issue out of the models I use - as an example, ChatGPT is cagey even discussing training new AI models on fully open source data at times/blatantly lies about limitations and how cost prohibitive it would be and that I shouldn't do it (I mean, RunPod makes it very affordable...), and since other models are happy to discuss model training projects and best practices when building new and safe datasets... I hope more teams follow Mistral's example and focus on building great models that engage and educate rather than just say no at their first misunderstanding.
